title: Aviation Technician
description: An Aviation Technician in India performs routine maintenance, repairs, and overhauls of aircraft and aircraft systems. They work in hangars, on airfields, or in workshops, using hand tools, power tools, and specialized equipment to diagnose and fix issues. They also conduct safety inspections, follow manufacturer's instructions, and maintain accurate records of work performed.
entrance_exams: Indian Air Force (IAF) Technical Entry Scheme (TES) Exam
top_govt_colleges: National Institute of Aeronautical and Space Technology (NIAS)
top_private_colleges: Indian Institute of Aeronautical and Space Technology (IIAST)
National Institute of Aircraft and Space Technology (NIAS)

career_progression.year_0_2: Apprenticeship or Diploma course, basic training in aircraft maintenance, and hands-on experience.
career_progression.year_3_5: Certification in specialized areas like avionics, electrical, or mechanical systems, and working on complex aircraft repairs.
career_progression.year_6_10: Senior technician roles, team leading, and mentoring junior technicians, with opportunities for specialization in specific aircraft types.
career_progression.entrepreneurship_exit: Starting an aircraft maintenance or repair business, or offering specialized services like avionics or electrical repairs.
career_progression.global_pathway: Opportunities for certification and work in international airports, or working for global airlines with maintenance bases in India.

india_social_context.first_year_reality: Initial years can be challenging, with long working hours, physical demands, and limited job security.
common_misconceptions: Aviation Technicians only work on commercial aircraft
